figures.caption: Table 1: Studies on competitive ratio analysis for drone routing applications
Figure 5: Observed competitive ratios σ̂ for Optimistic and Regretless on LARGE Note. The red line indicates analytical competitive ratios σ from Section 5.

Figure 6: Competitive ratios σ̂ for Optimistic and Regretless for several trucks and drones on SMALL Note. The red line indicates analytical competitive ratios σ from Section 5. The red line for α = 2 for Regretless depicts value 3 and is not visible in the figure.
Figure 7: Observed drone-impact ratios for Optimistic and Regretless on LARGE Note. The red lines indicates analytical drone-impact ratios ω̄ and ω from Section 5.

Figure 2: Example of an RDP∗ instance (full-information counterpart) Note. Left figure: An instance with α = 2, i.e., the drone is two times faster than the truck; damaged nodes D = {v1, v2, v3}, 1 truck, and 1 drone. Right figure: An optimal solution for this instance with makespan 17: the truck travels (v0, v1, v3, v2, v1, v0), the drone travels (v0, v1, v3, v5, v4, v1, v0).
